news_article_data_extraction_prompts:
From all the provided multilingual screenshots of a news website, identify and extract the headline and the full text of the main article.
 The headline is usually the largest text at the top, followed by a block of text.
 If the headline or text is overlaid on an image, consider the text as part of the main content. Translate any non-English headline or article text into English. 
Provide the extracted information in English language in the format provided below:
- Headline: [Extracted headline]
- Article_Text: [Extracted text]

From all the provided multilingual screenshots of a news website, extract the published date and the name of the publishing agency.
The published date might appear near the headline or at the end of the article. The publishing agency name is often located in the header or footer.
 Provide the extracted information in English language in the format provided below:
-Published_Date: [Extracted date in DD-MM-YYYY format]
- Publishing_Agency_Name: [Extracted name]

From all the provided multilingual screenshots of a news website, extract the name(s) of the author(s) or contributor(s) of the article.
    This information is often found near the headline or at the beginning or end of the article. Provide the extracted 
    information in English language in the format provided below:
-Author_Names: [Extracted names]

From all the provided multilingual screenshots of a news website, identify and extract any keywords or tags associated with the article.
    These might be located near the headline, at the end of the article, or within a sidebar. Provide the information in this format:
-Keywords_Tags: [Extracted keywords or tags]

From all the provided multilingual screenshots of a news website, extract any links or references to related articles, especially those 
    that might support or reinforce the content of the main article. Provide the extracted information in English language in 
    the format provided below:
    - Related Article Links: [Extracted links or titles of related articles]

Analyze the sentiment and tone of the article text in the screenshots of news website. Determine whether the language is 
    positive, negative, neutral, or persuasive. Provide the extracted information in English language in the format provided below:
- Sentiment: [Positive/Negative/Neutral/Persuasive]
- Tone: [Descriptive tone, e.g., Fear-mongering, Encouraging, etc.]

Identify the underlying narrative or message that the article is trying to convey, especially in relation to the EU election.
This could be a pro/anti-EU stance, promoting specific candidates or policies, or spreading specific conspiracy theories.
 Provide the extracted information in English language in the format provided below:
- Narrative: [Summary of the narrative or message]
advertisement_extraction_prompt:
For the provided multilingual screenshot of a news website, identify and extract details of all advertisements present, 
  regardless of their location on the page. Advertisements may appear in the top section before the headline, sidebar (left and right both sides of the website screenshot), banner, bottom sections, inline,
  or anywhere else on the page. Focus on text that might be overlaid on images and is associated with products, companies, or services. Ignore any areas where the ad is closed and showing a placeholder for Google ads, as well as online advertising advertisements.
Translate any non-English text into English.
  Provide the extracted information in English in the format provided below for each advertisement found:
    - Ad Name: [Extracted ad name]
    - Product: [Extracted product]
    - Company: [Extracted company]
    - Ad Description: [Extracted Ad description (what the advertisement is about)]
    - Ad Placement: [Location of the ad on the page, e.g., sidebar, banner, bottom, etc.]
